seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with Semantic Kernel enables sophisticated conversational agents using plugins, planners, and memory for enterprise-grade LLM integration. However, complex plugin chaining, invalid plans, memory hallucinations, and data contamination introduce critical failure modes that can degrade response quality and safety. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, coherence, safety, PII protection, and hallucination detection in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The Semantic Kernel AI Chatbot architecture centers on a Kernel orchestrating LLM interactions with plugins for intent classification, RAG retrieval, and multi-turn memory. User input flows through an intent classifier plugin to route to appropriate handlers like RAG for knowledge retrieval or memory recall for conversation history. Planners generate execution sequences across plugins, while memory stores and retrieves context for coherent multi-turn dialogues.

Key components include: IntentClassifierPlugin for routing, RAGPlugin for retrieval-augmented responses, ConversationMemory for state management, and a Planner to sequence plugin calls. The Kernel executes plans and aggregates results into final responses, making it vulnerable to cascading errors from plugin failures or contaminated data flows.

This setup excels for enterprise chatbots but requires verification at function boundaries to catch planning errors, hallucinated memories, and unsafe outputs before user exposure.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Sequential plugin failures propagate invalid data, causing planner replans or malformed responses
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each plugin result before passing to next in chain

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Planner selects incompatible plugin sequences or wrong parameters
risks.mitigation: Vex validates plan steps against schema and historical success rates

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Memory plugin fabricates conversation history, breaking coherence
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-verifies recalled memory against vector store and session state

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: RAG results leak into memory or intent classification corrupts planning
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces data isolation and schema validation between plugins

implementation.title: Basic Chatbot Kernel Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Semantic Kernel with core chatbot plugins for intent and RAG
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

kernel = Kernel()

# Add chat completion
kernel.add_chat_service(
    "chat-gpt", OpenAIChatCompletion("gpt-4", api_key="your-key")
)

# Simple intent plugin
kernel.import_plugin_from_prompt(
    "IntentClassifier",
    """Classify user intent: greeting, question, task"""
)

# RAG plugin
kernel.import_plugin_from_prompt(
    "RAG",
    """Answer using this context: {{context}} Query: {{question}}"""
)

print("Kernel ready for chatbot")

implementation.title: Multi-turn Memory Integration
implementation.description: Add conversation memory for context-aware responses
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory

memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=kernel.memory_store)

# Store conversation history
await memory.save_information_async(
    collection="chat_history",
    text="User: Hello, I'm building a chatbot",
    id="msg1",
    description="greeting"
)

# Recall relevant context
results = await memory.search_async(
    collection="chat_history",
    query="chatbot conversation",
    limit=3,
    min_relevance_score=0.7
)

context = "\n".join([r.metadata.text for r in results])

implementation.title: TypeScript Kernel with Plugins
implementation.description: TypeScript setup with native plugin functions

implementation.title: Planner for Plugin Orchestration
implementation.description: Configure planner to sequence intent → RAG → response
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning import ActionPlanner

planner = ActionPlanner(kernel)

request = "What's the capital of France?"

# Generate plan
plan = await planner.create_plan_async(request)

# Execute plan
result = await plan.invoke_async(
    input=request,
    intent="question",
    context="Use RAG for facts"
)

print(result)

vexIntegration.title: Add Vex Filter to Kernel Function Results
vexIntegration.description: Install Vex filter on plugin results to catch cascading errors early
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from semantic_kernel import KernelFunction

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

@kernel.register_function(
    plugin_name="IntentClassifier",
    name="classify",
    description="Classify intent"
)
async def classify_intent(context: KernelArguments) -> str:
    result = "question"  # plugin logic
    
    # Vex verification
    verified = await vex.verify({
        "input": context["input"],
        "output": result,
        "verifications": ["response_accuracy", "safety_filtering"],
        "risk_profile": "high"
    })
    
    if not verified:
        raise ValueError("Intent classification failed verification")
    
    return result

vexIntegration.title: Vex Memory Recall Verification
vexIntegration.code:
async def safe_memory_recall(query: str):
    # Recall from memory
    results = await memory.search_async(
        collection="chat_history", query=query
    )
    
    recalled_context = "\n".join([r.metadata.text for r in results])
    
    # Vex hallucination detection
    verification = await vex.verify({
        "type": "memory_recall",
        "recalled": recalled_context,
        "query": query,
        "verifications": ["hallucination_detection", "conversation_coherence"],
        "session_id": "chat_123"
    })
    
    return recalled_context if verification else "No verified context"
